mnemonics.title: KILL Signals Remember
mnemonics.content: K-I-L-L: K = Kill (SIGKILL, 9), I = Interrupt (SIGINT, 2), L = Quit (SIGQUIT, 3), L = Hangup (SIGHUP, 1) - All terminal-related signals

mnemonics.title: Core Dump Signals
mnemonics.content: Core dumps happen with: SIGQUIT(3), SIGILL(4), SIGABRT(6), SIGFPE(8), SIGSEGV(11). Remember: Q-I-A-F-S (Quit, Illegal, Abort, Floating-point, Segfault)

mnemonics.title: Signals That Stop
mnemonics.content: STOP, TSTP, TRAP, TTOU cannot be caught or ignored. Remember: S-T-T-T (Signals that Stop and can't be Stopped otherwise)

mnemonics.title: Signal Handler Setup
mnemonics.content: When catching signals: SAVE the old action (for later restoration), SETUP the new handler, RESTORE if needed. S-S-R mnemonic

acronyms.expansion: Signal Identifier - prefix for all signal names

acronyms.usage: SIGRTMIN to SIGRTMAX - queued signals with priority ordering
SA_RESTART, SA_NODEFER, SA_SIGINFO - modifiers for signal handling behavior
Returned by system calls when interrupted by a signal

visualTips.visualization: Draw a timeline: Event occurs → Signal generated → (if blocked, becomes pending) → Signal delivered → Handler executes (or default action)

Imagine signals as balls and the signal mask as a wall. Blocked signals bounce off and pile up (pending). When wall is lowered, all pending balls come through (but only one per type for standard signals).
Traffic light analogy: Green = Default action (go), Yellow = Custom handler (proceed with caution), Red = Ignore (stop completely)
Child dies → Parent doesn't wait → Child becomes zombie (living dead). If Parent sets SIGCHLD to SIG_IGN → Child is buried automatically (no zombie).

analogies.analogy: Like a fire alarm in a building. The alarm (signal) goes off, and people (processes) respond - some ignore it, some evacuate (default), some follow procedures (custom handler).

analogies.concept: Signal Mask as Do Not Disturb
analogies.analogy: Turning on 'Do Not Disturb' on your phone - notifications (signals) arrive but don't alert you. When you turn it off, you see pending notifications.
Synchronous is like tripping on a rug - you cause it yourself. Asynchronous is like someone calling your phone - external event, unpredictable timing.
Like an answering machine. When you're busy (handler set), callers (signals) get a recorded message (handler execution). If no answering machine (default), you answer or ignore.
